title: papaver somniferum subsp. somniferum
intro: Papaver somniferum subsp. somniferum, commonly known as the opium poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly the alkaloids present in its latex, including morphine, codeine, and thebaine, which have been used to produce pain-relieving medications. The opium poppy has various health benefits, including pain relief, cough suppression, and sedation, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Papaver somniferum subsp. somniferum include opium tincture, capsules, and topical creams, often used to treat conditions such as chronic pain, insomnia, and anxiety.
